title: High-level roundtable explores intertwined challenges of climate resilience, defence and security
content: On 26 May, the European Commission and the European External Action Service hosted a high-level meeting to discuss the links between climate resilience, security and defence.
snippet: On 26 May, the European Commission and the European External Action Service hosted a high-level meeting to discuss the links between climate resilience, security and defence.

title: European Commission opens call for proposal for grants to advance European IRIS² user terminal industrialisation
content: The European Commission launches a new call for proposals for grants to support the development of affordable user terminals for services under the Union secure connectivity programme - IRIS² - and GOVSATCOM.
snippet: The European Commission launches a new call for proposals for grants to support the development of affordable user terminals for services under the Union secure connectivity programme - IRIS² - and GO

title: Commission fines Temu
content: The European Commission issued a fine of €200 million to Temu under the Digital Services Act (DSA). The evidence at the disposal indicates that consumers in the EU are very likely to encounter illegal items on Temu. Temu has until 28 August 2026 to submit an action plan.
snippet: The European Commission issued a fine of €200 million to Temu under the Digital Services Act (DSA). The evidence at the disposal indicates that consumers in the EU are very likely to encounter illegal

title: Schengen short-stay Visa applications rise in 2025 but remain below pre-pandemic levels
content: According to figures released by the Commission, EU and Schengen-associated countries received nearly 12 million applications for short-stay visas in 2025, marking a 1.8% increase from 2024. However, demand remains well below the 17 million applications recorded in 2019 before the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: The EU makes good progress towards its 2030 CO₂ injection target
content: The European Commission has today published its first progress report on the EU CO2 injection capacity target set under the Net-Zero Industry Act, showing that the number of CO₂ storage sites in the European Union is on the rise.

title: Package travel - Stronger rights for travellers and simpler rules for the travel industry
content: The new rules adopted by the European Parliament and the Council strengthen traveller rights and improve legal certainty for the package travel sector, which is largely made up of SMEs and micro-enterprises.

title: New EAfA brochure: Good for Youth, Good for Business
content: EAfA is launching the fourth edition of its Good for Youth, Good for Business brochure, highlighting how high‑quality apprenticeships help young people to gain the skills they need, support business growth, and strengthen Europe’s competitiveness.

title: New EU Task Force on Fusion Energy
content: The European Nuclear Safety Regulators Group (ENSREG) new Task Force on Fusion Energy held its inaugural meeting on 26-27 May 2026 in Cadarache, France with representatives from Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Italy, Spain and Sweden, the European Commission and observers from the IAEA and the UK

title: Research and Innovation against Illicit Drugs - From Foresight to Impact
content: On 5-6 May, participants from law enforcement entities, customs, forensics, academia, civil society, Horizon Europe projects, EU agencies and institutions came together in Brussels for a CERIS workshop on illicit drugs. The event was organised by the Commission and the European Union Drugs Agency.

title: Reforming EU humanitarian aid to meet today’s global challenges
content: Disastrous humanitarian situations coupled with severe funding cuts mean re-prioritising how aid is delivered. To answer the challenges ahead, the European Commission will adapt its way of working to maximise the effectiveness and impact of EU humanitarian aid around the world.

title: Enlargement: Albania meets interim benchmarks for fundamentals cluster
content: Today, the eighth meeting of the Accession Conference with Albania confirmed that Albania has, overall, met the interim benchmarks on the so-called cluster 1: fundamentals. This cluster covers notably the functioning of democratic institutions, public administration reform, the rule of law chapters
snippet: Today, the eighth meeting of the Accession Conference with Albania confirmed that Albania has, overall, met the interim benchmarks on the so-called cluster 1: fundamentals. This cluster covers notably

title: Commission seeks feedback on fisheries multiannual plans
content: The European Commission is seeking feedback on fisheries Multiannual Management Plans (MAPs) across the EU’s sea basins. The call for evidence is open for a four-week period on the Commission’s Have Your Say portal.
snippet: The European Commission is seeking feedback on fisheries Multiannual Management Plans (MAPs) across the EU’s sea basins. The call for evidence is open for a four-week period on the Commission’s Have Y

title: CBD COP17: Addressing the biodiversity financing gap
content: Around 100 stakeholders gathered online and in Brussels to find innovative ideas on scaling up private investment in biodiversity.
snippet: Around 100 stakeholders gathered online and in Brussels to find innovative ideas on scaling up private investment in biodiversity.

title: Commission publishes guidance to strengthen Europe's resilience by clarifying the application of EU water laws
content: The new document ensures a more harmonised implementation of water laws by reducing compliance uncertainties, among other things.
snippet: The new document ensures a more harmonised implementation of water laws by reducing compliance uncertainties, among other things.

title: EU signs trade agreements with Mexico
content: Following the signature of the Modernised Global Agreement and the Interim Trade Agreement, the EU and Mexico follow their respective procedures to ratify the agreements.
snippet: Following the signature of the Modernised Global Agreement and the Interim Trade Agreement, the EU and Mexico follow their respective procedures to ratify the agreements.

title: EU and Mexico deepen long-term ties at a historic summit
content: Today, the EU and Mexico have taken a major step forward in their partnership by signing the Modernised Global Agreement (MGA) and interim Trade Agreement (iTA).
snippet: Today, the EU and Mexico have taken a major step forward in their partnership by signing the Modernised Global Agreement (MGA) and interim Trade Agreement (iTA).

title: Commission launches EUR 16.5 million call to dismantle criminal networks
content: The European Commission has published a call for proposals to co-fund projects aiming to fight organised crime and drug trafficking, as well as trafficking in human beings. With EUR 16.55 million, it will support law enforcement but also public bodies working to dismantle criminal networks.

title: Ten years of the GDPR: your data, your rights
content: The GDPR turns ten. Learn how this landmark data protection law changed life online forever, what rights it gives you, and how the EU protects you in the digital world.
snippet: The GDPR turns ten. Learn how this landmark data protection law changed life online forever, what rights it gives you, and how the EU protects you in the digital world.

title: Commission publishes 2025 report on the Digital Markets Act implementation
content: The Commission published its third annual report outlining the progress towards achieving the objectives of the DMA to support fair and contestable digital markets in the EU.
snippet: The Commission published its third annual report outlining the progress towards achieving the objectives of the DMA to support fair and contestable digital markets in the EU.

title: The Ocean Economy Imperative: Defining Value, Managing Risk and Mobilizing Investment
content: An Ocean Action Agenda Briefing Paper by the World Economic Forum aims to shift the conversation from awareness to action.
snippet: An Ocean Action Agenda Briefing Paper by the World Economic Forum aims to shift the conversation from awareness to action.

title: Get career support when you need it most
content: Recently lost your job, or just finished school? Read up on how to get help for your next career steps with the EU’s Youth Guarantee initiative.
snippet: Recently lost your job, or just finished school? Read up on how to get help for your next career steps with the EU’s Youth Guarantee initiative.

title: Cyprus sees developments in migrant access to health care and employment initiatives
content: Beneficiaries of international protection can now access the Cypriot health system, while updates to labour migration legislation clarify access to employment and support integration in the Cypriot society. Cyprus is also part of MPF-funded programmes delivering training in the countries of origin.

title: Commission discusses gas and oil supply risks with EU countries as the conflict in the Middle East reaches a 3-month mark
content: In a meeting of the Gas Coordination Group held on 26 May and an ad hoc meeting of the Oil Coordination Group held yesterday, the Commission and EU countries discussed the security of gas and oil supplies in the EU in light of the continued tensions in the Middle East.

title: Commission approves €15 million French State aid for agricultural and aquaculture companies facing increased fuel prices
content: The European Commission has approved a €15 million French State aid scheme to support agricultural and aquaculture companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis. The scheme was approved under the Middle East Crisis Temporary State Aid Framework (METSAF)
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €15 million French State aid scheme to support agricultural and aquaculture companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis. The scheme was

title: Commission opens in-depth foreign subsidies investigation into JD.com's proposed acquisition of CECONOMY
content: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess, under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation ('FSR'), the proposed acquisition by JD.com, Inc. ('JD.com') of CECONOMY AG ('CECONOMY').
snippet: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess, under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation ('FSR'), the proposed acquisition by JD.com, Inc. ('JD.com') of CECONOMY AG ('CECONOMY').

title: Commission opens consultation on territorial supply constraints
content: The European Commission has launched a public consultation to gather stakeholders and citizens' views on planned action to address territorial supply constraints.
snippet: The European Commission has launched a public consultation to gather stakeholders and citizens' views on planned action to address territorial supply constraints.

title: Inclusive transport for all: gender, migration and intersectionality in mobility
content: On 20 May, as part of EU Diversity Month 2026, the Ambassadors for #DiversityInTransport Network hosted the webinar “Inclusive transport for all: gender, migration and intersectionality in mobility”.
snippet: On 20 May, as part of EU Diversity Month 2026, the Ambassadors for #DiversityInTransport Network hosted the webinar “Inclusive transport for all: gender, migration and intersectionality in mobility”.

title: EU and Vietnam deepen partnership in science, technology and innovation
content: Research and innovation news alert: Tthe European Commission and Vietnam’s Ministry of Science and Technology signed a letter of intent to strengthen cooperation in science, technology and innovation.
snippet: Research and innovation news alert: Tthe European Commission and Vietnam’s Ministry of Science and Technology signed a letter of intent to strengthen cooperation in science, technology and innovation.

title: Commission launches call for evidence on digital action plan for water sector
content: The initiative will help shape EU-wide strategies that integrate smart technology across the industry.

title: EU signs Protocol amending the Council of Europe Convention on the prevention of terrorism strengthening global counter-terrorism efforts
content: On 26 May, the EU signed the Protocol amending the Council of Europe Convention on the Prevention of Terrorism, marking a historic step towards a common pan-European definition of terrorist offences, which is now fully aligned with the definition set out in the Directive on combating terrorism.
snippet: On 26 May, the EU signed the Protocol amending the Council of Europe Convention on the Prevention of Terrorism, marking a historic step towards a common pan-European definition of terrorist offences,

title: From local action to global impact: EU delivers on biodiversity ahead of CBD COP17
content: Protecting biodiversity is increasingly recognised not only as an environmental necessity, but also as an economic and security imperative.
snippet: Protecting biodiversity is increasingly recognised not only as an environmental necessity, but also as an economic and security imperative.

title: VAT in the Digital Age: 2026 Work Programme available
content: The European Commission has published the work programme for VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA), covering implementation activities planned for 2026.
snippet: The European Commission has published the work programme for VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA), covering implementation activities planned for 2026.

title: Commission approves €13 million French State aid for fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to Middle East crisis
content: Approved €13 million French aid scheme to support fishing companies to address the increase in fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis. The scheme was approved under the Middle East Crisis Temporary State Aid Framework (METSAF) adopted by the Commission on 29 April 2026.

title: Commission awards €400 million to European projects to decarbonise heat production
content: Today, the European Commission has selected 65 projects under the Innovation Fund Heat Auction, the first ever EU-wide auction to accelerate the deployment of innovative clean heat technologies across European industry.
